label: p10_memory_decode_optimization_pass_review

hardware.note: local diagnostics only; W7900 not rerun

scope: Tasks #61-#65 GGUF memory/decode optimization pass after PARO comparison.

accepted_changes.summary: Removed resident bulk-prefill scratch key/value caches and unused full_key_bf16 allocation.
accepted_changes.evidence: Targeted GGUF pytest bundle 12 passed; 32K/1 tracked peak 23.368929 -> 23.302035 GiB, saving 68.5 MiB.

rejected_or_deferred.evidence: 512/1 raw/no-repack saved 468 MiB but dropped prefill 1545 -> 117 tok/s and decode 53 -> 40 tok/s; keep T16 resident.
4K/128 decode regressed 96.755 -> 70.879 tok/s (-26.7%); source change reverted.

rejected_or_deferred.summary: Selected-MoE Q4 T16 launch-bounds tweak rejected.
rejected_or_deferred.evidence: 4K/128 decode 96.746 -> 96.651 tok/s (-0.10%); source change reverted. Hot path already fuses Q4 gate/up+SiLU; Q5/Q6 direct kernels already use 128-thread launch bounds.

next_actions_ranked: Measure/add Q4_K_S GGUF column; this is the largest likely memory lever because Q4_K_M carries 9.257 GiB of Q5/Q6/Q8 payload.
For memory beyond Q4_K_S, consider a granular/emergency no-T16 residency mode only if long-context is more important than prefill/decode speed.
For decode speed, avoid simple metadata-load tweaks; use structural selected-MoE grouping/fusion and Q8 dense/shared work mapping changes, ideally guided by rocprof/SASS.
For max-fit, re-probe GGUF 32K/128 and near-35K after Q4_K_S or any larger memory change; the 68.5 MiB scratch cleanup alone is not enough to move 128K.
rollup_reason: Diagnostic/local-only, mixed model/quant comparison, rejected probes retained only as artifacts, and no full retained evidence bundle for a new benchmark row.
